message:
fix(self-update): emit installer-fallback hint when self-update fails

When an older mcp-agent-mail binary (pre-v0.2.47) attempts self-update
and hits a download or replacement error, the failure message leaves
the user stuck — the in-binary updater has no way to bootstrap the
fixed updater from a broken one (GH#102).

Add a self_update_installer_fallback_hint(version) helper that renders
a copy-pasteable one-liner:

    curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/\
    mcp_agent_mail_rust/v<ver>/install.sh | bash -s -- --version v<ver> \
    --verify

Print it after both failure branches in handle_self_update_full:
- Download failed (before returning the error)
- Binary-replacement failed (after the staged-extract-dir message)

The installer path downloads and verifies release artifacts directly,
so it always works regardless of which binary invoked it. Success
paths are untouched.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
